title: Malva cisplatina
preparations: Malva cisplatina is not a valid scientific name for a plant species. However, I can provide information on Malva sylvestris, a similar plant. Herbal preparations from Malva sylvestris, often referred to as mallow, have been used in traditional medicine for their soothing and anti-inflammatory properties. Infusions or teas made from the leaves and flowers are said to be effective in treating sore throats, coughs, and digestive issues. Mallow preparations have also been applied topically to soothe skin irritations and wounds.
intro: I couldn't find any information on the plant Malva cisplatina.
